kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON:
{"filter":[{"$state":{"store":"globalState"},"meta":{"alias":null,"disabled":false,"field":"diagnostic.id","indexRefName":"kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON.filter[0].meta.index","key":"diagnostic.id","negate":false,"params":{"query":"elasticsearch-aws-prod@2025-02-10~b94e"},"type":"phrase"},"query":{"match_phrase":{"diagnostic.id":"elasticsearch-aws-prod@2025-02-10~b94e"}}}],"index":{"allowHidden":false,"allowNoIndex":false,"fieldFormats":{},"id":"96e1e8a9834447a3fd183fffb27106f0ee7c1421d916242a4b93b69bc2441f56","name":"metrics-shard-esdiag*","runtimeFieldMap":{},"sourceFilters":[],"timeFieldName":"@timestamp","title":"metrics-shard-esdiag*","type":"esql"},"query":{"esql":"FROM metrics-shard-esdiag* 
| KEEP index.name, shard.number, index.ilm.phase, node.tier, shard.store.total_data_set_size_in_bytes, index.age, diagnostic.id
| WHERE index.ilm.phase IS NOT NULL
| WHERE index.age > 0
| WHERE index.ilm.phase != node.tier
| SORT index.age DESC
| RENAME shard.number AS shard, shard.store.total_data_set_size_in_bytes AS store.size, index.ilm.phase AS ilm.phase
| LIMIT 100
"}}

Important Metrics\n\n* `Score` - An estimation of how good load is balanced\n    - Higher is better, from 1-5\n* `Max Deviations` - A statistical measure of load similarity\n    - Lower is better, good is below 1.5, ideal is below 1\n* `Load Ratio` - The load `max / min` where `1` is perfectly balanced\n    - Lower is better, ideal of `1`\n\n#### Explanation\n\nThis is load is not exclusive to ingest or indexing workloads, but the node's overall workload *strongly* influences indexing performance.\n\nThe node load balance is the unix operating system's `load average` metric. This is a composite number influenced by CPU, storage, and network usage. A value of 1 indicates a single CPU core is at full capacity either doing work (`user`/`sys`) or waiting on input/output operations (`iowait`).\n\nLoad averages above the core count, for example a `load_average.1m` of `11.4` on a system with 8 CPU cores means for the last `1m` the node was _overloaded_. If `1m` is lower than `15m` load was decreasing; if `1m` is higher than `15m` then load was Important Metrics\n\n* `Score` - An estimation of how good indexing pressure  is balanced\n    - Higher is better, from 1-5\n* `All Max Dev` and `Coord Max Dev` How well balanced is indexing pressure\n    - Lower is better, good is below 1.5, ideal is below 1\n\n#### Explanation\n\nThe `indexing_pressure` metrics measure the current amount of heap memory used for indexing operations. This is broken down into four categories:\n\n1. `coordinating` - Memory used for routing indexing requests\n2. `primary` - Indexing for primary shards\n3. `replica` - Indexing for replica shards\n4. `all` - Total of the above\n\nIn an ideal balance, the `primary + replica` indexing pressure on all nodes will be similar.\n\nSeparately, the `coordinating` pressure should also be similar across all nodes. Where `primary` and `replica` require shards to be allocated to that node, the `coordinating` pressure does not; _any_ node can coordinate a request.\n\nIf there are nodes with _no_ `coordinating` pressure, this could be an indicator of poor load balancing or network misconfiguration.","openLinksInNewTab":false},"title":"","type":"markdown","uiState":{}}},"gridData":{"h":30,"i":"032b93ad-5a33-4843-a527-f82e34e4528a","w":24,"x":24,"y":48},"panelIndex":"032b93ad-5a33-4843-a527-f82e34e4528a","type":"visualization"},{"embeddableConfig":{"attributes":{"references":[{"id":"837271ec-b73e-4fb3-b7e4-190dba34bbf5","name":"indexpattern-datasource-layer-220c42f4-de5e-47e5-ac22-9664b99fad93","type":"index-pattern"}],"state":{"adHocDataViews":{},"datasourceStates":{"formBased":{"currentIndexPatternId":"837271ec-b73e-4fb3-b7e4-190dba34bbf5","layers":{"220c42f4-de5e-47e5-ac22-9664b99fad93":{"columnOrder":["21c95355-324b-4271-9ff2-80017671b3aa","21c95355-324b-4271-9ff2-80017671b3aaX0","21c95355-324b-4271-9ff2-80017671b3aaX1","21c95355-324b-4271-9ff2-80017671b3aaX2","21c95355-324b-4271-9ff2-80017671b3aaX3"],"columns":{"21c95355-324b-4271-9ff2-80017671b3aa":{"customLabel":true,"dataType":"number","isBucketed":false,"label":"Score","operationType":"formula","params":{"format":{"id":"number","params":{"decimals":0}},"formula":"pick_max(floor(6-1.5*(max(node.indices.indexing.index_total)-average(node.indices.indexing.index_total))/standard_deviation(node.indices.indexing.index_total)+0.5),1)","isFormulaBroken":false},"references":["21c95355-324b-4271-9ff2-80017671b3aaX3"],"scale":"ratio"},"21c95355-324b-4271-9ff2-80017671b3aaX0":{"customLabel":true,"dataType":"number","isBucketed":false,"label":"Part Elastic Stack Diagnostics (ESDiag) is designed to simplify collecting, processing, and analyzing the health of your deployment.

This Kibana instance is connected to an Elasticsearch cluster hosting all of the processed diagnostics data. Start at the `Diagnostics List` on the left to find the most recently processed diagnostics, and start an analysis.","openLinksInNewTab":false},"title":"","type":"markdown","uiState":{}},"title":""},"gridData":{"h":28,"i":"72cf1871-ce7b-493e-bbb7-b2c2888c3b6c","w":20,"x":8,"y":3},"panelIndex":"72cf1871-ce7b-493e-bbb7-b2c2888c3b6c","type":"visualization"},{"embeddableConfig":{"enhancements":{"dynamicActions":{"events":[]}},"savedVis":{"data":{"aggs":[],"searchSource":{"filter":[],"query":{"language":"kuery","query":""}}},"description":"","params":{"fontSize":12,"markdown":"### Questions and Feedback

Join us in [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/elastic/esdiag/discussions). Engaging on the public forum helps other users find already answered questions and contribute to ideas.

### Enhancements and Bugs

For enhancements or bugs to the `esdiag` or `esdiag-control` command-line tools, including data enrichment or metrics pre-calculation, open a `Feature Request` in [github.com/elastic/esdiag/issues](https://github.com/elastic/esdiag/issues).

For enhancements or issues related to dashboards, saved searches, or anything Kibana related, please create an `Dashboard Request` in [github.com/elastic/esdiag/issues](https://github.com/elastic/issues).
